title: Add a student
summary: Add a student in Inserting Data

sketchBlocks.title: Why INSERT matters
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The `INSERT` statement lets you add new rows to a table. For example, to add a new inventory item:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Notebook', 'Accessories', 3.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10');
```

It's best practice to list the columns explicitly, so your data goes into the right places, even if the table structure changes later.

sketchBlocks.title: Required columns and defaults
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Some columns require values, while others have defaults or allow NULLs. For example, in `inventory_items`, the `status` column defaults to `'draft'` if not specified. The `notes` column can be left NULL. If you omit a required column without a default, the database will return an error.

sketchBlocks.title: Checking your insert
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
After inserting a row, you can check your work:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE name = 'Notebook';
```

This helps confirm that your new data appears as expected.

quizDraft.title: Insert a new inventory item
quizDraft.prompt: Add a new row to the `inventory_items` table for an item named 'Student Badge' in the 'Accessories' category, priced at 2.50. Set status to 'active', is_test to 0, notes to NULL, and last_updated to '2026-02-10'. Use an explicit column list and do not specify the id.
quizDraft.hint: List all columns except id in your INSERT statement. Use NULL for notes.

help.concept: When inserting, it's best to specify all columns you want to set, except for those with auto-increment or defaults. Use NULL for columns that allow it.
help.hint_1: Write INSERT INTO inventory_items (column1, column2, ...) VALUES (...).
help.hint_2: Remember to use 'Student Badge' for name, 'Accessories' for category, and set notes to NULL.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Student Badge', 'Accessories', 2.50, 'active', 0, NULL, '2026-02-10');

quizDraft.title: Insert with default values
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new item called 'Sample Lanyard' in the 'Accessories' category, priced at 5.00. Only specify name, category, and price. Let all other columns use their defaults.
quizDraft.hint: Only list the columns you want to set. The rest will use their default values.

help.concept: If you omit columns in your INSERT statement, the database will use default values or NULL if allowed.
help.hint_1: List only name, category, and price in your INSERT INTO statement.
help.hint_2: Do not include status, is_test, notes, or last_updated in your column list.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Insert only name, category, and price
quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price)
VALUES ('Sample Lanyard', 'Accessories', 5.00);

quizDraft.title: Insert multiple rows
quizDraft.prompt:
Insert two new rows into `inventory_items` in a single statement:
1. 'Test Eraser', 'Accessories', 1.25, status 'draft', is_test 1, notes 'Test batch', last_updated '2026-02-11'
2. 'Trial Pen', 'Accessories', 1.50, status 'draft', is_test 1, notes 'Trial batch', last_updated '2026-02-11'
Do not specify id. Use an explicit column list.
quizDraft.hint: You can insert multiple rows by separating each VALUES set with a comma.

help.concept: SQL allows inserting multiple rows at once by providing multiple sets of values in a single INSERT statement.
help.hint_2: Each set of values should match the column list and be separated by a comma.

quizDraft.starterCode: -- Insert two rows in one statement

quizDraft.title: Choosing columns for INSERT
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following statements about INSERT are true? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about best practices and what happens if you omit columns.

help.concept: Explicitly naming columns in INSERT statements helps prevent errors and makes your code more robust. Omitting required columns without defaults causes errors.
help.hint_1: Consider what happens if you don't specify all columns, or if you rely on defaults.
help.hint_2: Some columns require values, others have defaults or allow NULL.

options.a: It's safer to list columns explicitly in INSERT statements.
options.b: Omitting a required column without a default will cause an error.
options.c: You must always specify every column in the table.
options.d: You can insert NULL into columns that allow it.

quizDraft.title: Default values in INSERT
quizDraft.prompt: When you omit the 'status' column in an INSERT into `inventory_items`, what value will it have by default?
quizDraft.hint: Check the table definition for the default value of 'status'.

help.concept: Columns can have default values set in the table schema. If you don't provide a value, the default is used.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT keyword in the schema for 'status'.
help.hint_2: The default for 'status' is set in the CREATE TABLE statement.
